claim: The D20 geon candidate is certified here only as a finite deterministic mask/sandpile audit: robust oblongness is already witnessed, the rendered state channels carry nonzero entropy and structured spectra, and the alpha channel is constant opaque in the stored renderer. The report explicitly does not promote a physical geon, alpha/subpixel entropy, or weight-8 Hamming-coordinate claim.

certified_here: the finite D20 voltage-lift render is a deterministic opaque mask field
the stored D20 lift is backed by the certified robust-oblongness theorem
rendered D20 and square-grid state fields have nonzero state/RGB entropy
the final rendered state spectra are far from histogram-preserving shuffles
alpha/subpixel entropy is absent from the current stored renderer
not_certified_here: three independent weight-8 Hamming-code coordinate systems

definition.geon_candidate_reading: a finite coherent mask/sandpile candidate: robust oblongness plus structured state-channel spectra, not a promoted continuum or physical geon theorem
definition.object_under_audit: the finite D20 voltage-lift sandpile render and its square-grid Abelian comparison embedded in generated/d20_sandpile_visualization.html
definition.rgba_boundary: the stored renderer writes RGB state palettes and an opaque alpha channel; no current repo artifact stores subpixel or alpha-phase entropy

next_highest_yield_item: Export actual RGBA frame buffers from the D20 lift and square-topple canvases, then rerun this audit on saved frame arrays. That is the direct witness needed to test the alpha/subpixel phase claim instead of inferring it from the current renderer.

open_obligations.needed_witness: archive real RGBA frame buffers, not just the deterministic HTML renderer, before asserting alpha/subpixel phase entropy
construct explicit coordinate maps and weight-8 Hamming-code checks; the current Golay-Hamming probes record this boundary as unresolved
supply a semantics bridge from the finite mask/sandpile object to a physical or continuum geon model

stage_protocol.closure: validate hashes, simulations, entropy/spectrum checks, and explicit demotions
stage_protocol.coherence: compare the replay with certified robust-oblongness and Golay/Hamming boundary reports
stage_protocol.draft: treat the geon language as a candidate reading of existing D20 sandpile/lift artifacts
stage_protocol.emit: emit a proof-obligation report plus the next highest-yield witness target
stage_protocol.witness: replay the deterministic HTML D20 lift and square Abelian simulations for fixed frames

d20_hex_lift_canvas.relationship: same D20 state field, rendered through the regular-hex comparison projection

d20_lift_canvas.rgba_write_rule: ImageData RGB from D20_LIFT_PALETTE with image.data[offset + 3] = 255

square_topple_canvas.rgb_write_rule: opaque CSS hex colors in TOPPLE_COLORS; no alpha-valued state channel

family_survival_summary.conclusion: The spheroid geometry survives the tested natural lifts, but the dramatic oblongness is coordinate-amplified by the current cooriented sign/selector lift.
coordinate_dependent_features: anisotropy magnitude varies from about 1.35x to about 5.60x
principal-axis angle shifts from about -41.3 degrees to -60 degrees
the cooriented selector sign lift is the strongest oblong projection among the tested variants
robust_features: all tested natural lifts have a single oblong covariance ellipse with anisotropy ratio greater than 1
all tested principal axes have negative slope in the chosen screen coordinates
the qualitative spheroid/ellipse behavior survives coordinate choice
